q: A client calls the support desk and says this to a technician: "I have just started my computer and attempted to check e-mail. The error 0x800ccc0d was displayed. However, I was able to connect to network drives and access the Internet." Which statement would describe the status of this issue?
a: The helpdesk has enough information to begin finding a resolution.

q: A construction firm is building a power plant at a remote and isolated mine site and needs to connect the data network of the plant to the corporate network. Which public technology is the most suitable to meet the connection needs?
A general term for Internet services from a mobile phone or from any device that uses the same technology
A network administrator issues the telnet www.cisco.com 25 command on the workstation. What is the purpose of this command?
a: to probe the server that provides the web service to determine if it is also running an email service

q: A network architecture that virtualizes the network

A network engineer is investigating an access issue to a web server. The web server can access the Internet and the local clients can open the web pages. However, outside clients cannot access the web pages. What is the most likely cause of the problem?
a: An ACL is blocking incoming connections.

q: A network engineer is troubleshooting a network that has recently been updated with a new routing protocol, but the network is not working as expected. The engineer is comparing the running configuration from before and after the change was made. Which approach to troubleshooting the problem is the engineer using?

The physical serial interfaces have also been enabled for PPP multilink. Which additional command should to be issued on the multilink interface?

a type of network that establishes a dedicated circuit (or channel) between nodes and terminals before the users may communicate.
After gathering symptoms, if the troubleshooter determines that the problem is located outside the control of the organization, what is the next step?
a: Contact the administrator of the external system.

Packets are classified into different flows based on header information including the ToS value.

SDN is a network architecture that has been developed to virtualize the network.

The show ip interface brief and show ipv6 interface brief commands are used to display the up or down status and IP address of all interfaces on a device.

The show ip route and show ipv6 route commands are used to display the routing table in a router to learn the directly connected neighbors, more remote devices (through learned routes), and the routing protocols that have been configured.
The show monitor command is used to verify the SPAN session.
The use of BGP is most appropriate when an AS has connections to multiple autonomous systems.

topology shows the physical layout of the devices connected to the network.

Under which condition does congestion occur on a converged network with voice, video, and data traffic?
a: if the request for bandwidth exceeds the amount of bandwidth available

q: What happens when an edge router using IntServ QoS determines that the data pathway cannot support the level of QoS requested?
a: Data is not forwarded along the pathway.

q: What happens when the memory queue of a device fills up and new network traffic is received?
a: he network device will drop the arriving packets.

q: What is a characteristic of a WAN?
a: WAN service providers include carriers such as a telephone network or satellite service.

q: What is a characteristic of data flow processing in SDN?
a: Each data flow through the network must be approved by the SDN controller first.

q: What is a characteristic of the two-tier spine-leaf topology of the Cisco ACI fabric architecture?
a: The leaf switches always attach to the spines, but they never attach to each other.

What is an example of an M2M connection?
a: a sensor in a garbage can signaling that it is full and delivering the data to a GPS mapping system for the sanitation truck to adjust its route for pickups

q: What is an SNMP management agent?
a: software that is installed on devices managed by SNMP

q: What is Fog computing?
a: It is a type of computing where services are hosted where they are used, such as at the network edge or with end devices.

a: at the same time each day across a set period of average working days, so that typical traffic patterns can be established

q: Which statement describes a characteristic of standard IPv4 ACLs?
a: They filter traffic based on source IP addresses only.

q: Which statement describes a difference between the operation of inbound and outbound ACLs?
a: Inbound ACLs are processed before the packets are routed while outbound ACLs are processed after the routing is completed.

q: Which statement describes the physical topology for a LAN?
a: It defines how hosts and network devices connect to the LAN.

q: Which statement describes the QoS classification and marking tools?
a: Marking is the adding of a value to a packet header.

a: NCP interfaces with Layer 3; LCP interfaces with Layer 1
